macro_summary: U.S. stocks sold off on Wednesday as the May CPI came in at 4.2% YoY — the highest since April 2023 — matching expectations but stoking rate‑hike bets. Oil jumped 3.8% to $91.93 after Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz closed and President Trump warned of new strikes. The VIX surged 11.8% to 22.22, and the SOXX fell 3.1%, extending its 5‑day loss to -12.1%.
commodities_note: WTI climbed 3.8% to $91.93 on the highest‑level Hormuz closure announcement. Gold dropped 2.8% to $4,089 as real yields held firm. The dollar was flat, and the U.S. 10‑year yield edged up to 4.54%.

singapore.summary: Singapore banks remain in WATCH, with none offering a clear entry trigger. The NIM‑compression thesis persists as the U.S. 10‑year yield holds well above the 4.20% reset threshold. OCBC's relative strength (1.9:1 R:R) stands out, but DBS and UOB lack any edge — the cluster needs either a rate breakdown or a MAS signal.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally challenged — dual NIM compression from US10Y >4.20% and MAS S$NEER appreciation persists.
singapore.key_development: No new MAS or rate relief today; OCBC remains the lone name with a near‑actionable setup at SGD 23.29, while DBS/UOB are stuck with 1:1 R:R.

semiconductors.summary: The Semis cluster absorbed another heavy blow, with SOXX falling 3.1% to 541.5 — now 12% down over five sessions. Oversold leaders NVDA and TSM gave way to BUY signals, but extreme extension still blocks AMD (+21%), MU (+37%), and 000660.KS (+35%). The bifurcation is stark: frontline names are pricing in a recession, while the peripherals haven't fully reset.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish (AI capex), tactically oversold for leaders but extreme extension blocking peripheral names.
semiconductors.key_development: NVDA upgraded to BUY after reclaiming the 50‑day; AAPL/AMD/MU remain deep in CAUTION on extension. SOXX at 541.5 is testing the 200‑day average — a breach would signal a regime shift.

big_tech.summary: BigTech diverged sharply. GOOG was upgraded to BUY on oversold conditions and TPU momentum, while MSFT continues to suffer from Azure concentration fears and China layoffs, and AMZN is caught in a capex‑overhang trap at RSI 32.5. PLTR's 160% valuation premium keeps it firmly in avoidance.
big_tech.thesis_status: Structurally mixed — cloud growth sustains, but rate and regulatory headwinds cloud the outlook.
big_tech.key_development: GOOG upgraded to BUY; MSFT Azure China cuts add geopolitical risk to an already‑clouded revenue quality story.

interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if Coherent pulls back 8‑10% from current levels alongside LITE, confirming optics‑sector entry timing.

interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC design wins (Amazon Trainium, Google Axion) make it a direct AVGO competitor — hyperscaler ASIC spend accelerates.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: AI datacenter networking upgrades benefit Arista — NOK's AI networking thesis shares read‑through on hyperscaler switch spend.
interconnected.entry_note: Becomes interesting if AI capex commentary from hyperscalers in July confirms 800G/1.6T switch demand; wait for a pullback to the 200‑day if rates rise.

active_risks: Iran declares Strait of Hormuz closed after fresh U.S. strikes — sharp oil supply shock (Reuters, June 10).
May CPI 4.2% YoY meets expectations but highest since 2023; rate‑hike odds surge, Fed hold cemented until year‑end (CNBC, Reuters).
Pentagon expands China military blacklist to include Alibaba, Baidu, BYD — escalates U.S.-China tech decoupling (KUNC, Newsweek).
Trump threatens not to renew USMCA trade deal — North American trade uncertainty adds a tariff tail‑risk (NYT).
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil surged 3.8% to $91.93, pressuring tech stocks (QQQ -1.8%) and lifting volatility (VIX +12%). The rate‑hike shift from the CPI print hit semis hardest (SOXX -3.1%), while gold fell 2.8% as real yields firmed. The Singapore banks underperformed slightly on the day, with volume light across all three — the rate differential keeps NIM pressure intact.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Iran officially declared the Strait of Hormuz closed, and President Trump announced new strikes — a significant escalation from prior de‑escalation signals. CPI matched the 4.2% estimate, but the core print kept rate‑hike bets alive.

base.description: Hormuz stays effectively closed, WTI oscillates near $90‑95. The Fed holds but rate‑hike chatter caps growth stocks. Semis stabilize around SOXX 530‑550, but extreme extension tickers (AMD, MU) remain blocked. Singapore banks hold current levels.

optimistic.description: A ceasefire or partial reopening of the strait sends WTI back toward $85, easing inflation fears. Tech bounces sharply; NVDA and GOOG build on their BUY signals, and the Singapore banks rally as the U.S. 10‑year drops toward 4.3%.

pessimistic.description: The conflict expands, oil breaks $100, and the CPI‑fueled rate hike becomes reality. Semis crash through the 200‑day average, and all BUY signals are cut. Singapore banks drop toward structural support as a recession scare hits.

wildcard.description: A sudden U.S.-Iran deal reopens the strait, oil crashes to $75, and equity volatility reverses violently. The semis short‑squeeze is massive, but the rate‑hike narrative remains — a short‑lived rally.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold existing BUY positions in NVDA, GOOG, and BE with half‑sizing; no new entries until the VIX retreats below 20 or oil stabilizes below $88. The Singapore banks are a wait‑and‑see; any pullback to SMA50 levels is a buying opportunity.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensive — risk‑off on Iran escalation and CPI, but selective BUY signals emerge in NVDA, GOOG, and BE.
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 8 of 27 names are hard‑blocked on technical extension; rising oil and a hawkish Fed re‑pricing keep the majority of the watchlist in avoidance. The three BUY signals are fragile — half‑sizing is essential.

taxonomy_discrimination.note: Output-only meta-analysis of signal-class discrimination. NOT injected into the analysis prompt and NOT persisted into memory.lessons_learned. full_corpus pools pre-cutover (different-instrument) rows — informational only.

calibration_insights.confidence_banner: Actionable buckets BUY (n=4) / ACCUMULATE (n=11) spanning chop, trend_up. NOT yet decision-grade — alpha figures are benchmark-relative; single-regime samples carry no out-of-regime evidence.
calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop.
